---
title: "Orthogonal Correction using PC1 of Batch and Acquisition Effects"
author: "Tecla Duran Fort"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  github_document:
    toc: yes
  pdf_document:
    toc: yes
    fig_caption: yes
  html_document:
    toc: yes
    df_print: paged
always_allow_html: yes
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      fig.path = "pc1_joint_correction_files/figure-latex/")
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(reshape2)
source("../../load_gcims_tools.R")
```

# 1. Load Data

```{r load-data}
df <- read.csv("../../data/peak_table_var.csv")

# Intensity matrix
X <- as.matrix(df %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("Cluster")))
```

# 2. Design Matrices

```{r design-matrices}
# One-hot encoding for batch
B <- model.matrix(~ 0 + factor(df$batch))
colnames(B) <- paste0("Batch_", sort(unique(df$batch)))

# Acquisition index per batch
df <- df %>%
  arrange(batch, elapsed_time) %>%
  group_by(batch) %>%
  mutate(acquisition_index = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup()

# One-hot encoding for acquisition index
A <- model.matrix(~ 0 + factor(df$acquisition_index))
colnames(A) <- paste0("Acq_", 1:max(df$acquisition_index))
```

# 3. PCA of Batch and Acquisition Reconstructions

```{r pca-batch-acquisition}
# Batch means and PCA
X_batch_means <- B %*% (solve(t(B)%*%B) %*% t(B) %*% X)
pca_batch <- prcomp(X_batch_means, scale. = TRUE)
pc1_batch <- pca_batch$x[,1, drop=FALSE]

# Acquisition means and PCA
X_acq_means <- A %*% (solve(t(A)%*%A) %*% t(A) %*% X)
pca_acq <- prcomp(X_acq_means, scale. = TRUE)
pc1_acq <- pca_acq$x[,1, drop=FALSE]
```

## Visualize PC1 Scores

```{r pc1-plot, fig.width=15, fig.height=4, echo=FALSE}
scores_batch_df <- data.frame(Sample = 1:nrow(X),
                              PC1 = pca_batch$x[,1],
                              Batch = df$batch)
scores_acq_df <- data.frame(Sample = 1:nrow(X),
                            PC1 = pca_acq$x[,1],
                            Acquisition = df$acquisition_index)

p1 <- ggplot(scores_batch_df, aes(x = Sample, y = PC1, color = factor(Batch))) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1), alpha = 0.6, color = "grey30") +
  labs(title = "PC1 scores from Batch-reconstructed data",
       y = "PC1", color = "Batch") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 15)
  )

p2 <- ggplot(scores_acq_df, aes(x = Sample, y = PC1, color = Acquisition)) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5) +
  geom_line(aes(group = 1), alpha = 0.6, color = "grey30") +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "G", name = "Acquisition") +
  labs(title = "PC1 scores from Acquisition-reconstructed data",
       y = "PC1") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 15)
  )

p1 + p2
```

# 4. Orthogonal Correction

```{r joint-correction}
# Apply correction jointly with both directions as external variables
joint_corr <- orthogonal_correction(X, cbind(pc1_batch, pc1_acq))

X_corr_joint <- joint_corr$corrected
proj_joint   <- joint_corr$projection
```

# 5. Visualization of Correction

```{r correction-visualization, fig.width=7, fig.height=2.5, echo=FALSE}
example_cluster <- X[, 18]   # pick one signal for illustration
signal_mean <- mean(example_cluster)

df_vis <- data.frame(
  Sample = 1:length(example_cluster),
  Original = example_cluster - signal_mean,
  Projection = proj_joint[,18],
  Corrected = X_corr_joint[,18] - signal_mean
)

y_limits <- range(df_vis)

p1 <- ggplot(df_vis, aes(x = Sample, y = Original)) +
  geom_line(color = "#253494", size = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Original Signal", y = "Centered Intensity") +
  ylim(y_limits)

p2 <- ggplot(df_vis, aes(x = Sample)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Original), color = "grey50", size = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Projection), color = "#e9c46a", size = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Projection (PC1 Batch + PC1 Acquisition)", y = "Centered Intensity") +
  ylim(y_limits)

p3 <- ggplot(df_vis, aes(x = Sample, y = Corrected)) +
  geom_line(color = "#006d2c", size = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Corrected Signal", y = "Centered Intensity") +
  ylim(y_limits)

p1 
p2
p3
```

# 6. PCA Before and After Correction

```{r pca-corr, fig.width=10, fig.height=4, echo=FALSE}
# PCA on original and corrected data
pca_orig <- prcomp(scale(X))
pca_corr <- prcomp(scale(X_corr_joint))

expl_orig <- summary(pca_orig)$importance[2,1:2] * 100
expl_corr <- summary(pca_corr)$importance[2,1:2] * 100

axis_labels_orig_x <- paste0("PC1 (", round(expl_orig[1],1),"%)")
axis_labels_orig_y <- paste0("PC2 (", round(expl_orig[2],1),"%)")

axis_labels_corr_x <- paste0("PC1 (", round(expl_corr[1],1),"%)")
axis_labels_corr_y <- paste0("PC2 (", round(expl_corr[2],1),"%)")

df_orig <- data.frame(PC1 = pca_orig$x[,1], PC2 = pca_orig$x[,2],
                      Batch = factor(df$batch),
                      Acquisition = df$acquisition_index)
df_corr <- data.frame(PC1 = pca_corr$x[,1], PC2 = pca_corr$x[,2],
                      Batch = factor(df$batch),
                      Acquisition = df$acquisition_index)


```

```{r pca-orig, fig.width=10, fig.height=4, echo=FALSE}
df_orig$elapsed_time <- df$elapsed_time

# Arrows
gradient_arrow_centered <- function(x, y, v, frac = 0.28) {
  dx <- cov(x, v, use = "complete.obs")
  dy <- cov(y, v, use = "complete.obs")

  rx <- diff(range(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  ry <- diff(range(y, na.rm = TRUE))

  len <- sqrt((dx/rx)^2 + (dy/ry)^2)
  if (is.na(len) || len == 0) return(data.frame(x0=NA,y0=NA,x1=NA,y1=NA))

  sx <- (dx/rx) / len
  sy <- (dy/ry) / len

  xc <- mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  yc <- mean(y, na.rm = TRUE)

  hx <- 0.5 * frac * rx * sx
  hy <- 0.5 * frac * ry * sy

  data.frame(
    x0 = xc - hx, y0 = yc - hy,
    x1 = xc + hx, y1 = yc + hy
  )
}

batch_num <- as.numeric(as.character(df_orig$Batch))

arr_batch <- gradient_arrow_centered(df_orig$PC1, df_orig$PC2, batch_num)
arr_time  <- gradient_arrow_centered(df_orig$PC1, df_orig$PC2, df_orig$elapsed_time)

navy <- "#0b3c5d"

p_orig_batch <- ggplot(df_orig, aes(PC1, PC2, color = Batch)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_segment(
    data = arr_batch,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linetype = "Increasing batch"),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 1.8,
    color = navy,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.32, "cm"))
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(name = "", values = c("Increasing batch" = "solid")) +
  labs(x = axis_labels_orig_x, y = axis_labels_orig_y) +
  theme_minimal()

p_orig_time <- ggplot(df_orig, aes(PC1, PC2, color = elapsed_time)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "G", name = "Elapsed Time") +
  geom_segment(
    data = arr_time,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linetype = "Increasing elapsed time"),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 1.8,
    color = navy,
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.32, "cm"))
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(name = "", values = c("Increasing elapsed time" = "solid")) +
  labs(x = axis_labels_orig_x, y = axis_labels_orig_y) +
  theme_minimal()

p_orig_combined <- (p_orig_batch + p_orig_time) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "PCA of original data",
    subtitle = "Colored by external variables",
    tag_levels = "A",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
    )
  )

p_orig_combined
```

```{r pca-corrected, fig.width=10, fig.height=4, echo=FALSE}
df_corr$elapsed_time <- df$elapsed_time

p_corr_batch <- ggplot(df_corr, aes(PC1, PC2, color = Batch)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(x = axis_labels_corr_x, y = axis_labels_corr_y) +
  theme_minimal()

p_corr_time <- ggplot(df_corr, aes(PC1, PC2, color = elapsed_time)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "G", name = "Elapsed Time") +
  labs(x = axis_labels_corr_x, y = axis_labels_corr_y) +
  theme_minimal()

p_corr_combined <- (p_corr_batch + p_corr_time) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "PCA of corrected data",
    subtitle = "Colored by external variables",
    tag_levels = "A",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
    )
  )

p_corr_combined
```
